stem: A hiking trail is 3 miles long. There are 5,280 feet in 1 mile. What is the length of the trail in feet?
explanation:
To convert miles to feet, multiply the number of miles by 5,280 feet per mile.

$$3 \text{ miles} \times \frac{5{,}280 \text{ ft}}{1 \text{ mile}} = 15{,}840 \text{ ft}$$

The miles unit cancels, leaving feet. The trail is 15,840 feet long.

distractorRationale.A: Wrong conversion direction: divides 5,280 by 3 instead of multiplying ($5{,}280 \div 3 = 1{,}760$). This inverts the operation, as if the student tried to find how many miles are in 5,280 feet split among 3 equal parts.
distractorRationale.B: Stops one step early: reports only the conversion factor (5,280 feet per mile) without multiplying by the given distance of 3 miles.
distractorRationale.D: Uses a wrong conversion factor: substitutes 5,300 ft per mile (rounding 5,280 up to the nearest hundred) giving $3 \times 5{,}300 = 15{,}900$ ft. This reflects a student who misremembers the conversion as approximately 5,300 feet per mile.
